name: Applebaum, Herman S.
description: With a 90-yard handicap he could easily place first in the 100-yard dash, and that, too, in spite of the fact that he measures in height almost the length of one of Wagner's shanks. In addition to this, "Happy" enjoys the distinction of possessing an ethical turn of mind. Indeed, some of his answers have been known to elicit from the Dean his familiar "Oh, dear me; no! How could you say that?"

name: Belt, Donald K.
clubs: Manager of The News-Letter (So., J., S.)
Captain and Manager Tennis Team (J., S.)
Chairman Permanent Banquet and Reunion Committee
description: Every breath a gasp, every step a toddle, every ounce of his rotund person a reservoir of energy, every instant an opportunity to work, every friend a possible victim to be roped into some scheme for improving the University—that's Donald. The best known and least understood, most talked about and least considered, most generous and most gouging, most popular and most hated member of the class. Misogynist, money-maker, business manager of things-in-general.

name: Benedict, Bertram
description: What will the world do when Benedict is gone? This question suggests Bertram's opinion of himself. Ours? We can summarize all we think of him by comparing him to a millstone: he grinds well.

name: Brown, William Norman
description: Lest you mistake the bellicose cast of his features for a real invitation to battle, we warn you that he is a perfectly harmless old fake.

name: Buckler, Leslie Hepburn
description: Behold the embodiment of the philosophy of Omar Khayyam. Give Leslie a jug of wine, a tree, and a girl, and he is satisfied. You can even omit the girl and the tree; but, for heaven's sake, don't forget the jug.

name: Burgan, John Kuper
description: "Johnny's" head is small, but it contains a deal of logical thought. "Johnny's" work troubles him much. "If I didn't have the lacrosse team on my hands," argues "Johnny," "I could give more attention to my studies; and if I didn't have my studies, I could give more attention to the lacrosse team." Poor little fellow!

name: Carroll, John Dudley
description: The only fault we have to find with Dudley is that he doesn't wear his wings. Otherwise he is perfectly fitted for the angelic existence; for, like most angels, he is little seen, and heard still less.

name: Coblentz, Maurice Henry
description: The old tradition that ministers' sons always turn out bad has received a body blow. Our "Coby" is such a model youth that it is a foregone conclusion that he will convert the unregenerate Medical School and carry the civilizing influences of Levering Hall to Darkest Broadway.

name: Cole, Harry Norman
clubs: charter member of the Dorcas Society
description: The only co-ed in the class; popularly known as "Sister"; charter member of the Dorcas Society. He has given up his plan of converting the heathen, in order to sell candy to the sweet young things on Broad Street, Richmond.

name: Corner, James Johnson
motto:
"When Mary to our banquet went,
She wore her nice full dress;
When Mary had that evening spent,
She was a perfect mess!"
description: This nice young man is so fond of seltzer water that he bathes in it whenever he is able.

name: Dickey, Harry Stanley
description: Harry is an expurgated edition of Hyland Stewart. When Stewart frowns, Harry frowns, too. If Stewart makes an attempt at a joke, Harry laughs uproariously. We wonder what Harry will do when he gets out into the cold, cold world, and Stewart is not near to point the way.

name: Du Bois, Hamilton Graham
description: "Yon" Graham has a lean and hungry look. He rhymes too much. This rarified individual has deluded himself into thinking that he is a born poet. Heaven save the mark! Our only fear is that his success as the author of our class play may encourage him to continue in this unwholesome frame of mind.

name: Easter, James W.
description: "Jimmie's" universe is the ladies, and his moral standard is the greatest happiness of the greatest number (of ladies, to be sure!) He hasn't as yet announced his political affiliations, but anyone with half an eye can see that he is a bred-in-the-bone Suffragist.

name: Fulton, John Samuel, Jr.
clubs: Delegate to Athletic Board (F., So., J.)
description: Fulton is Jack-of-all-trades and master of none. What "Jack" lacks is concentration. Not concentration on self. As the Dean would say: "Oh, dear; no!"

name: Gail, George William
description: "Bill's" theology is the exact reverse of the usual doctrines. His conception of Heaven is a place where there shall be no Bliss; his notion of the other place is a region paved not with good intentions, but with villainous Ames. Anyhow, the girls all say he's an angel on the lacrosse field.

name: Gorton, Arthur Feddeman
description: Gorton has long been suspected of having a dual personality. Indeed, nature seems to have fitted him for the double role. That lofty brow is wisdom's outward form; and so successfully has he used this disguise that it has won him the Phi Beta Kappa key. But the keys to the hearts of the fair Are the locks of his pompadour hair.

name: Grempler, Walter Edward
description: Get out your microscope. For four years this little fellow has been known to make but one impression, and that on a photographic plate. So far he has never done any harm except to pool-balls; but as he is said to entertain the idea of entering the medical profession, it is hoped that this book will serve as a timely warning to many an unsuspecting patient.

name: Hatch, Alfred Cummins
description: There was a young student named Hatch; The girls thought him quite a fine catch. He was treated with awe For his knowledge of law, And he learned everything with dispatch.

name: Holmes, James Eugene Levering
description: Jimmy is the class philanthropist, but he doesn't believe in Holmes for the Friendless. It was rumored that he was elected to the Fire Department to throw cold water where the high pressure doesn't reach.

name: Hopkins, Marion Byrd
description: Yes, dear reader, it is really alive. At times Hop's face has been known to take on an expression of human intelligence. Also, the Dean and Dr. Watson have succeeded in calling forth sounds. "Don't punch me, if they ask me a question," he warned the man back of him. "I can't hear them!"

name: Huck, John Gardiner
description: Athletics and banquets are John's chief amusements. He is a dead shot with a siphon bottle at a table length's distance; and his marksmanship has won him the position of Hopkins representative of the Associated Distillers, Brewers, and Vintners.

name: Lee, Frederic Collins
description: How does he do it? He is "some" fast—on the track and in the drawing-room. Which does he prefer—the cinder path or the carpeted stair, the spiked shoe or the dancing pump?

name: Lloyd, Howard Huntley
description: YOUNG LADIES PLEASE NOTICE! Huntley is guaranteed under the Pure Food and Drug Law to contain 99.99 per cent. sweet disposition. He is mama's model boy, and we have every assurance that he ain't never smoked, chewed, drank, cussed, or went to the theater. Besides, he looks perfectly grand in a running suit, and is just as musical as he can be. No bids accepted after June 15.

name: Miller, Ellis
clubs: Chairman Committee on Arrangements, Interclass Debate (S.)
description: The less said the better. Miller's qualities are originality and brilliancy. But what is original is not brilliant, and what is brilliant is not original.

name: Paulus, Max G.
description: Thith, ladieth and gentlemen, ith Maxth. He comth from the wildth of Highlandtown and frequenth the detherted parth of Thaint Paul threet, there amuthin himthelf rolling baby carriageth for a certain—"What did you thay her name wath, Maxth?"

name: Price, Thomas Brooke
clubs: Hopkins' Representative, Maryland Intercollegiate Oratorical Contest (S.)
description: This is an AWFUL subject to write on. And why need we say more? He likes to speak for himself (and others). His name indicates that he is valuable. But what's in a name?

name: Rogers, Lindsay
clubs: University Correspondent: The Baltimore American (So., J., S.)
Third in Lake Mohonk International Arbitration Essay Contest (J.)
description: Brass, bluff, bluster; Nerve, noise, nuisance; Conglomeration of aspirations and abominations.

name: Schmeisser, Gerhard Lauts
description: We never think of Schmeisser without thinking of cheese. His name has that pleasant connotation. Gerhart has a profound contempt for the English language. He can never speak a sentence without having his words tumble somersaults over one another. Some day, we hope, the floodgates of speech may be opened.

name: Rosenfeld, Moses Wiesenfeld
description: Speckless, spotless, sporty "Rosey;" Smiling, laughing, giggling, "Mosey;" When he lifts his feet, Oh, ain't they neat, Those noisy, striped, silken hosey. Breathes there the man with soul so dead Who never to himself hath said This is my own, my honored class, Who would not lend the slightest aid To class designs, one renegade 'Midst forty men? 'Tis so, alas!

name: Thomas, George Peter
description: Here is a man who is afraid to be as good as he really is. He is dominated by the Bucklerian principles. When "Pete" does anything naughty he always does so with an expression that says: "Just watch me. I'm doing this because you're looking at me." And we watch, and wonder, and smile.

name: Sutton, Alan C.
description: Sutton belongs to that select class of quiet students who saw lots of wood. He has elected to pursue graduate work in biology, and the subject of his thesis, as tentatively stated, is "AN INVESTIGATION INTO THE LENGTH OF WAGNER'S LEGS, WITH A VIEW TO ESTABLISHING ON A FIRMER BASIS, DARWIN'S THEORY OF NATURAL SELECTION."

name: Todes, Gabriel H.
description: While "Gaby" hasn't as yet disclosed his plans for the future, it is whispered that he intends to engage as a clown with Barnum & Bailey's. We sincerely believe that in so doing he will have realized his highest self, although the Dean might conceivably hesitate to pronounce this a summum bonum.

name: Wagner, Harry Milton Jr.
description: The human ostrich; an animated pair of stilts; the only living manifestation of a single dimension. It is rumored that he will step into "Johnny" Holmes' shoes; if he takes over the rest of the costume, he might use the trousers for running pants.

name: Weyforth, William Oswald Jr.
description: "Bill" is the fire-eating orator of the class. You could guess his enthusiasm from the animated expression of his countenance, but one would never suspect the fund of wisdom that modest head conceals. He rarely treats the class to his opinion. What is the use of casting pearls before swine?

name: White, Edwin Charles
description: White is the only well preserved and successfully resuscitated mummy that ever hailed from Grecian ruins. He is an early disciple of Diogenes, but unlike his famous preceptor, makes no use of the lantern, preferring rather to regard all men as scheming hypocrites and unconvicted grafters. Still, he has a few good points, and the Committee offers a prize of five dollars to anyone discovering them before doomsday. (Clue—See Mrs. Stewart.)

name: Applebaum, Herman S.
motto: "Oh, dear me; no! How could you say that?"
description: With a 90-yard handicap he could easily place first in the 100-yard dash, and that, too, in spite of the fact that he measures in height almost the length of one of Wagner's shanks. In addition to this, "Happy" enjoys the distinction of possessing an ethical turn of mind. Indeed, some of his answers have been known to elicit from the Dean his familiar "Oh, dear me; no! How could you say that?"
